 {"id":"gt-d46","title":"Mail CLI: archive, purge, search, mark","description":"GGT mail CLI needs more commands for mail management.

## Commands to Add

### gt mail check
Check for new mail without full inbox display.
```
gt mail check [--quiet] [--inject]
```
- --quiet: Only output if new mail (for scripts)
- --inject: Send notification to running session

### gt mail mark
Change read status.
```
gt mail mark <id> --read
gt mail mark <id> --unread
```

### gt mail delete
Remove message from inbox.
```
gt mail delete <id> [--force]
```
- Confirm unless --force

### gt mail archive
Move old/read messages to archive.
```
gt mail archive [--older-than DAYS] [--all-read] [--dry-run]
```
- Creates inbox.jsonl.archive or separate archive.jsonl

### gt mail purge
Permanently delete archived messages.
```
gt mail purge [--older-than DAYS] [--dry-run] [--force]
```

### gt mail search
Find messages by content.
```
gt mail search <query> [--from SENDER] [--subject] [--body]
```

### gt mail reply
Reply to a message.
```
gt mail reply <id> -m BODY
```
- Auto-sets reply_to and thread_id
- Auto-addresses to original sender

## Files to Modify
- internal/cmd/mail.go: Add commands
- internal/mail/mailbox.go: Add Archive(), Search(), Delete()

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All commands implemented
- [ ] Archive stores in separate file
- [ ] Search supports regex patterns
- [ ] Delete confirms by default","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:46:57.158136-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-16T16:04:32.922813-08:00"}

+{"id":"gt-dd8s","title":"gt molecule seed: create built-in molecules as beads","description":"The molecule infrastructure is complete but built-in molecules (engineer-in-box, quick-fix, research) need to be seeded into the beads database.

## Current State
- `gt molecule list` works but shows 0 molecules
- BuiltinMolecules() in internal/beads/builtin_molecules.go has 3 molecules defined
- No way to create them as beads

## Needed
Add `gt molecule seed` command that:
1. Reads BuiltinMolecules()
2. Creates each as a bead with type: molecule
3. Uses well-known IDs (mol-engineer-in-box, mol-quick-fix, mol-research)
4. Idempotent (skip if already exists)

## Acceptance Criteria
```
gt molecule seed
gt molecule list   # Shows 3 built-in molecules
```

- Update CLAUDE.md templates for refinery agents
- Keep `gt refinery` commands as-is (they manage the place)
- Internal code stays `internal/refinery/` (the module)

 {"id":"gt-h5n.7","title":"Auto-target: MRs for epic children target integration branch","description":"Automatically target integration branches for epic children.

When 'gt mq submit' is called:
1. Parse source issue from branch
2. Check if issue has a parent epic
3. Check if integration/<epic> branch exists
4. If yes: set target=integration/<epic>
5. If no: set target=main

This ensures batch work automatically flows to integration branches.

Also update 'gt mq submit --epic' to explicitly target an epic's integration branch.

 {"id":"gt-h5n.8","title":"MQ config schema: merge_queue section in rig config.json","description":"Define and implement merge_queue configuration in rig config.

Schema:
{
  "merge_queue": {
    "enabled": true,
    "target_branch": "main",
    "integration_branches": true,
    "on_conflict": "assign_back",  // or "auto_rebase"
    "run_tests": true,
    "test_command": "go test ./...",
    "delete_merged_branches": true,
    "retry_flaky_tests": 1,
    "poll_interval": "30s",
    "max_concurrent": 1
  }
}

Implement:
- Config loading in rig package
- Default values
- Validation

Reference: docs/merge-queue-design.md#configuration","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T13:52:00.322779-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T12:56:15.211099-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T01:33:49.864359-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-h5n.8","depends_on_id":"gt-h5n","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-17T13:52:00.324781-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-h5n.9","title":"Per-epic config overrides: custom merge settings","description":"Allow per-epic merge configuration overrides.

Epic can specify merge_config in its description:
  merge_config:
    run_tests: true
    test_command: 'go test -race ./...'
    on_conflict: assign_back

When processing MRs for an epic:
1. Load rig-level merge_queue config
2. Check if epic has merge_config
3. Merge epic config over rig config
4. Use merged config for processing

Use cases:
- Risky refactors: more thorough testing
- Urgent fixes: skip some checks
- Experimental work: different test suite

After a swarm completes, tearing down 20 polecats one at a time is tedious.
Ephemeral workers should be easy to create and destroy in bulk.

 {"id":"gt-hgk","title":"Mail system: message types and threading","description":"GGT mail system needs message types and threading like PGT.

## 1. Message Types
Add to internal/mail/types.go:
```go
type MessageType string
const (
    TypeTask         MessageType = "task"         // Required processing
    TypeScavenge     MessageType = "scavenge"     // Optional first-come work
    TypeNotification MessageType = "notification" // Informational
    TypeReply        MessageType = "reply"        // Response to message
)

// Update Message struct
type Message struct {
    // existing fields...
    Type     MessageType `json:"type"`
    ThreadID string      `json:"thread_id,omitempty"`
    ReplyTo  string      `json:"reply_to,omitempty"`
}
```

## 2. Priority Levels
Expand from 2 to 4:
```go
type Priority string
const (
    PriorityLow    Priority = "low"
    PriorityNormal Priority = "normal"
    PriorityHigh   Priority = "high"
    PriorityUrgent Priority = "urgent"
)
```

## 3. CLI Updates
internal/cmd/mail.go:
- Add --type flag to send: `gt mail send ... --type task`
- Add --reply-to flag: `gt mail send ... --reply-to <msg-id>`
- Add thread command: `gt mail thread <thread-id>`

## 4. Threading Logic
NewMessage() should auto-generate thread_id if not a reply.
Reply messages inherit thread_id from original.

## Files to Modify
- internal/mail/types.go: Add types, expand Priority
- internal/mail/mailbox.go: Thread filtering
- internal/cmd/mail.go: CLI flags and thread command

## PGT Reference
gastown-py/src/gastown/mail/message.py

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Messages have type field (default: notification)
- [ ] 4 priority levels supported
- [ ] Reply creates thread with shared thread_id
- [ ] gt mail thread <id> shows conversation","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T14:46:55.29463-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T12:56:15.211543-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-18T20:14:28.308997-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-v5k","title":"Design: Failure modes and recovery","description":"Document failure modes and recovery strategies for Gas Town operations.

## Critical Failure Modes

### 1. Agent Crash Mid-Operation

**Scenario**: Polecat crashes while committing, Witness crashes while verifying

**Detection**:
- Session suddenly gone (tmux check fails)
- State shows 'working' but no session
- Heartbeat stops (for Witness)

**Recovery**:
- Doctor detects via ZombieSessionCheck
- Capture any recoverable state
- Reset agent state to 'idle'
- For Witness: auto-restart via supervisor or manual gt witness start

### 2. Git State Corruption

**Scenario**: Merge conflict, failed rebase, detached HEAD

**Detection**:
- Git commands fail
- Dirty state that won't commit
- Branch diverged from origin

**Recovery**:
- gt doctor reports git health issues
- Manual intervention recommended
- Severe cases: remove clone, re-clone

### 3. Beads Sync Conflict

**Scenario**: Two polecats modify same issue

**Detection**:
- bd sync fails with conflict
- Beads tombstone mechanism handles most cases

**Recovery**:
- Beads has last-write-wins semantics
- bd sync --force in extreme cases
- Issues may need manual dedup

### 4. Tmux Failure

**Scenario**: Tmux server crashes, socket issues

**Detection**:
- All sessions inaccessible
- "no server running" errors

**Recovery**:
- Kill any orphan processes
- tmux kill-server && tmux start-server
- All agent states reset to idle
- Re-spawn active work

### 5. Claude API Issues

**Scenario**: Rate limits, outages, context limits

**Detection**:
- Sessions hang or produce errors
- Repeated failure patterns

**Recovery**:
- Exponential backoff (handled by Claude Code)
- For context limits: session cycling (mail-to-self)
- For outages: wait and retry

### 6. Disk Full

**Scenario**: Clones, logs, or beads fill disk

**Detection**:
- Write operations fail
- git/bd commands error

**Recovery**:
- Clean up logs: rm ~/.gastown/logs/*
- Remove old polecat clones
- gt doctor --fix can clean some cruft

### 7. Network Failure

**Scenario**: Can't reach GitHub, API servers

**Detection**:
- git fetch/push fails
- Claude sessions hang

**Recovery**:
- Work continues locally
- Queue pushes for later
- Sync when connectivity restored

## Recovery Principles

1. **Fail safe**: Prefer stopping over corrupting
2. **State is recoverable**: Git and beads have recovery mechanisms
3. **Doctor heals**: gt doctor --fix handles common issues
4. **Emergency stop**: gt stop --all as last resort
5. **Human escalation**: Some failures need Overseer intervention

## Implementation

- Document each failure mode in architecture.md
- Ensure doctor checks cover detection
- Add recovery hints to error messages
- Log all failures for debugging","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T23:19:07.198289-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T23:19:28.171942-08:00"}

Customize tmux status line for Gas Town workers with:
1. Per-rig configurable color themes
2. Clear worker name and role visibility

## Current Problem

- Only mayor shows in status line (and truncated)
- Can't tell which rig/worker you're looking at
- All sessions look the same

## Proposed Design

```yaml
# In rig config or beads
theme:
  primary: '#ff6600'    # Orange for gastown
  secondary: '#333333'
  accent: '#ffcc00'
```

### Status line format
```
[gastown/Rictus] polecat | gt-70b3 | branch: polecat/Rictus
[beads/emma] crew | working | branch: main  
[mayor] coordinator | idle
```

### Components
- Rig name with rig color
- Worker name
- Role (polecat/crew/mayor/witness/refinery)
- Current issue or status
- Branch name

## Configuration

 {"id":"gt-vci","title":"Mayor handoff mail template","description":"Add MAYOR_HANDOFF mail template to templates.py.

## Template Function

def mayor_handoff(
    active_swarms: List[SwarmStatus],
    rig_status: Dict[str, RigStatus],
    pending_escalations: List[Escalation],
    in_flight_decisions: List[Decision],
    recent_actions: List[str],
    delegated_work: List[DelegatedItem],
    user_requests: List[str],
    next_steps: List[str],
    warnings: Optional[str] = None,
    session_duration: Optional[str] = None,
) -> Message:
    metadata = {
        'template': 'MAYOR_HANDOFF',
        'timestamp': datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
        'session_duration': session_duration,
        'active_swarm_count': len(active_swarms),
        'pending_escalation_count': len(pending_escalations),
    }
    # ... format sections ...
    return Message.create(
        sender='mayor/',
        recipient='mayor/',
        subject='Session Handoff',
        body=body,
        priority='high',
    )

## Metadata Fields

- template: MAYOR_HANDOFF
- timestamp: ISO format
- session_duration: Human readable
- active_swarm_count: Number of active swarms
- pending_escalation_count: Number of escalations

## Mail Priority

Use priority='high' to ensure handoff is seen on startup.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T20:15:30.26323-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T20:48:59.550689-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-vci","depends_on_id":"gt-u82","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T20:15:39.554108-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-vdp0","title":"Crew workers getting wrong CLAUDE.md (shows Refinery)","description":"## Problem

Crew workers (emma, dave) have CLAUDE.md that says they're the Refinery.

## Evidence

```
$ head -5 /Users/stevey/gt/beads/crew/emma/CLAUDE.md
# Claude: Beads Refinery
You are the **Refinery** for the **beads** rig...
```

## Expected

Should use crew.md.tmpl which correctly says:
```
# Crew Worker Context
You are a **crew worker** - the overseer's (human's) personal workspace...
```

## Impact

- Crew workers have wrong identity in static context
- `gt prime` correctly outputs crew context, but CLAUDE.md conflicts
- Confusing role information

## Fix

Check `gt crew create` or whatever populates CLAUDE.md for crew workers.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-18T21:40:56.518032-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T01:33:49.863259-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T01:33:49.863259-08:00","close_reason":"Merged to main in swarm session","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-vdp0","depends_on_id":"gt-l4gm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T21:50:04.955247-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-vjv","title":"Add bulk session stop command (gt session stop --all)","description":"When decommissioning a rig, need to stop multiple sessions one at a time. A --all or --rig flag would allow: gt session stop --rig gastown","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-18T11:33:33.394649-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T11:38:51.399298-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-18T11:38:51.399298-08:00","close_reason":"Wrong model: streaming doesn't need bulk shutdown commands - polecats request their own shutdown"}
